intro: Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and Jony Ive's io Products, alleging that former Apple employees stole confidential hardware trade secrets and technical specifications for unreleased products. OpenAI's head of safety, Johannes Heidecke, departed the company this week, continuing a pattern of recent executive turnover that includes the departures of Chief Futurist Joshua Achiam and AGI head Fidji Simo. Sam Altman highlighted OpenAI's newest model release as the company faces intensifying competition, with rival AI firms releasing their own latest models this week.

whitelist.title: AI - Business Insider
whitelist.desc: Explore cutting-edge AI insights and trends with Business Insider's dedicated artificial intelligence coverage. Stay ahead with in-depth articles, expert analyses, and the latest news on AI technologies shaping industries worldwide.

whitelist.title: Apple accuses OpenAI, and former design star Jony Ive's io Products firm, of stealing hardware trade secrets in blockbuster lawsuit | Fortune
whitelist.desc: The lawsuit alleges that two former Apple employees now working at OpenAI stole confidential data including information about unreleased hardware products and technical specifications.

whitelist.title: OpenAI’s head of safety is out.
whitelist.desc: Turnovers at that ChatGPT maker continue. The company’s chief futurist, Joshua Achiam, and AGI head, Fidji Simo, announced their departures earlier this week. Now Johannes Heidecke, who has overseen OpenAI’s safety efforts, is out, as ChatGPT 5.6 rolls out and raises some alarms. [Media: https://twitter.com/zeffmax/status/2075750608745972148?s=46&t=fRkDIqgNCkTkvg8ZBiLA9A]

whitelist.title: What smart people are saying about Apple's lawsuit accusing OpenAI of stealing trade secrets
whitelist.desc: Apple sued OpenAI on Friday, accusing the AI startup of orchestrating a campaign to steal trade secrets from iPhone maker.

whitelist.title: The volatile AI trade marched higher, but oil kept Wall Street on edge last week
whitelist.desc: Chip stocks had a wild but strong week. Meta led our portfolio higher.

whitelist.title: The biggest bombshells from Apple's trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI
whitelist.desc: In a filing, Apple says former employees working for OpenAI retained access to the iPhone maker's systems after leaving.

whitelist.title: AI is entering a new phase — and these are the stocks investors should be watching

whitelist.title: Sam Altman Boasts Newest OpenAI Model In Latest Dig Against Elon Musk
whitelist.desc: Altman and Musk’s respective artificial intelligence firms both released their latest AI models this week.

whitelist.title: OpenAI engineer’s ‘LOL’ moment set stage for legal fight with Apple | Fortune
whitelist.desc: Most of OpenAI’s Apple recruits came from Ternus’ hardware engineering division, and some designers had backed Tan over Ternus for the top hardware job in 2021.

whitelist.desc: Intercept, a new nonprofit, will focus on countering the common cold and the flu.

whitelist.title: OpenAI folds safety into research as head of safety exits
whitelist.desc: Johannes Heidecke is leaving OpenAI as the company merges safety and research under a new VP role. It's the latest in a long line of safety exits and team disbandments.

whitelist.title: Google’s new Magic Pointer Play Store listing reveals a Gemini shortcut built for Googlebooks
whitelist.desc: Google’s newly surfaced Magic Pointer listing reveals a Gemini-powered cursor for Googlebooks, but incompatible Android devices mean the feature remains an early preview rather than something consumers can try today.

whitelist.desc: Apple claims OpenAI used current and former employees to steal hardware secrets as it builds an iPhone rival. Over 400 ex-Apple staff now work at OpenAI.

whitelist.title: OpenAI’s Greg Brockman will keep heading up product at the company.
whitelist.desc: Brockman had taken over product when Fidji Simo went on medical leave, and he’ll continue those responsibilities now that Simo is stepping down from her full-time OpenAI job and becoming a “part-time advisor,” CNBC reports. [Link: OpenAI power consolidates under co-founder Greg Brockman ahead of prospective IPO | https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/10/openai-power-consolidates-under-co-founder-greg-brockman-ahead-of-ipo.html | CNBC]

whitelist.title: U.K. agency finds 'universal jailbreaks' unlock dangerous cyber capabilities of OpenAI's GPT-5.6 | Fortune
whitelist.desc: The jailbreaks appear at least as severe as those that led to export controls on Anthropic's Fable model.

whitelist.title: Meta turns off the Instagram feature that let users make AI deepfakes of public accounts
whitelist.desc: Meta is turning off the feature it announced this week that let users generate AI images based on content from public Instagram accounts just by tagging them.

whitelist.desc: Apple alleges OpenAI improperly obtained trade secrets tied to the iPhone maker’s hardware development through former Apple employees as the AI company expands into consumer devices.

whitelist.title: AI Ransomware Is Here, Now Powered By Cheaper, Agentic Models
whitelist.desc: JadePuffer, a ransomware operation, used an AI agent to carry out much of the intrusion chain from reconnaissance, key theft, database encryption and even ransom note generation.

whitelist.title: Google's SensorFM Reveals Where AI Takes Wearable Health
whitelist.desc: Google's trillion-minute SensorFM data shows wearables shifting from raw metrics to AI health agents, a trend already reshaping Whoop and Oura.

whitelist.desc: Meta launched then pulled a feature letting anyone generate AI images of public Instagram users who were opted in by default.
